title: phlegmariurus talpiphilus
intro: Phlegmariurus talpiphilus, also known as the mole-loving horsetail fern, is a type of evergreen epiphytic plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Phlegmariurus talpiphilus include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the leaves to use as a topical poultice or added to capsules for oral consumption.
